name: Jack McDuff
description: American jazz organist and organ trio bandleader (1926-2001)
abstract: Eugene McDuffy (September 17, 1926 – January 23, 2001), known professionally as "Brother" Jack McDuff or "Captain" Jack McDuff, was an American jazz organist and organ trio bandleader. He was most prominent during the hard bop and soul jazz era of the 1960s, often performing with an organ trio.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz on the Hammond
Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz on the Hammond

works.t: I Want to Talk About You

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: For the next two-and-a-half years I was with Jack... I did six or seven more after that, with Jack.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Etta has collaborated with such greats as ... Jack McDuff ... both in concert and on recordings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: when I was working with Brother Jack McDuff... I joined his band
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Magnarelli
about_quotes.evidence: worked with Brother Jack McDuff I think... Well with Lionel Hampton and Jack McDuff, both... I learned how to be a man.

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: And it was at that point where I realized I really wanted to be a player... a thing that doesn't exist anymore, these kind of apprenticeships in places, especially for a guitarist, especially now that all the organ players are dying. I mean Jack McDuff’s gone, Groove Holmes is gone, Johnny’s gone.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: Jack and I met in Chicago... he had a small band that we used to work with and rehearse... McDuff recorded about three or four songs of mine, and then I recorded some other music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
